description_long: This airplane features a sleek, angular design with a dark blue fuselage and sharply swept wings, giving it a futuristic, almost stealth-like appearance. The nose is pointed and painted in a contrasting red, with a green-tinted cockpit canopy that appears to be forward-mounted and enclosed. The wings are mid-mounted with pronounced anhedral angles and are equipped with winglets that angle downward, enhancing aerodynamic stability. Twin vertical stabilizers are mounted at the rear, flanking the tail section, and the undercarriage shows multiple landing gear units, suggesting a robust landing system. The engines are integrated into the main body, with intakes visible beneath the wings, indicating a jet propulsion system. The overall form is compact yet aggressive, with multiple fins and protrusions that contribute to its advanced, tactical look.
description: A futuristic, dark blue airplane with swept mid-mounted wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a forward green-tinted cockpit. The design is angular and aggressive, with red accents and integrated jet engines.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, green cockpit canopy, and wide, swept wings with downward-angled winglets.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the angular fuselage, forward cockpit, and the pronounced anhedral wing configuration.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the twin vertical stabilizers, engine exhausts, and the full span of the swept wings.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the cockpit placement, wing structure, and tail fin arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept wings, pronounced anhedral angle, and downward-angled winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the fuselage beneath the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a conventional tailplane arrangement.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single green-tinted canopy.
